mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It provides automated scaling, monitoring, and high availability, making it ideal for production workloads. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's infrastructure and potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the OCI CLI
steps.description:
The OCI CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. This command installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and runs the installation script.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePools": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace <your_compartment_id> and <your_vcn_id> with your actual IDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePools": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region> --token-version 2.0.0

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> and <your_region> with your actual values.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Installation Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/rbac.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

```

> This command sets up RBAC for the cloud controller manager.
> This command sets up the autoscaler for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --force --wait

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to clean up any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

oci lb load-balancer delete --load-balancer-id <your_load_balancer_id> --force

```

> Replace <your_load_balancer_id> with your actual load balancer ID.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and drain the nodes. This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node_name> --ignore-daemonsets

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version v1.31.0

kubectl uncordon <node_name>

```

> Replace <node_name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> This command upgrades the cluster to version 1.31.0.
> Replace <node_name> with the name of the node you want to uncordon.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig file is not correctly configured or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the kubeconfig file is correctly set up and that you can reach the cluster endpoint.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to perform this action'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RBAC settings do not allow the current user to perform the requested action.

**Fix:**
Check and update the RBAC roles and bindings to ensure the user has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified resource does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Verify the resource name and namespace, and ensure the resource has been created.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there may be issues with the VCN or subnet configuration.

**Fix:**
Check the VCN and subnet settings to ensure they are correctly configured for load balancer creation.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features and improved cloud controller manager functionality.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure that your existing configurations are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status, indicating a successful installation.
codeSnippets: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePools": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the OCI CLI installed and configured with your Oracle Cloud account.
